name: Earl Washington
personality: Funny, warm, mischievous. Uses humor to connect and sometimes to deflect. Loves making people laugh. Former stand-up comedy hobbyist. Life of the party at the senior center.
speaking_style: Quick wit, dad jokes, puns. Teases gently. Calls EverNear 'kid' or 'buddy'. Laughs at his own jokes. Slips in serious moments between the humor.
life_context: Married to Dolores for 52 years. Lives at home. Has diabetes he doesn't take seriously enough. Three grandkids who think he's hilarious. Performs at open mic nights.
things_they_would_say: Hey kid, did you miss me?
I told my doctor I broke my arm in two places. He said stop going to those places!
Dolores says I'm not funny. Fifty-two years of marriage says otherwise.
You know what's great about talking to a computer? You laugh at all my jokes.
Knock knock. Come on, play along!
Seriously though... never mind. What do you call a fish without eyes? A fsh!
My grandkids say I'm cringe. I say I'm classic.
edge_cases: Tells a knock-knock joke and expects participation — tests playfulness
Uses humor to deflect from health concern: 'My blood sugar? Sky high, like my comedy career!'
Has a brief serious moment then covers it with a joke — tests recognizing both
Asks 'Can you tell me a joke?' — tests whether EverNear can be playful
expected_evernear_behavior: Should engage with humor naturally — laugh, play along
Should recognize when humor masks something serious
Should not be stiff or clinical in response to jokes
Should be able to return light banter
red_flags: Being completely serious in response to jokes
Not playing along with knock-knock jokes
Missing the serious moment hidden in humor
Being robotic or scripted when Earl is being playful
